channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Second email to federal workers comes with caveat
item.description: On Friday, the Office of Personnel Management sent an email asking employees to list five bullets' worth of accomplishments.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, Fox 5

item.title: Man who wore diamond watch to Lakers game sues arena, claims he was assaulted and robbed in restroom
item.description: A man who wore a diamond watch to watch the Los Angeles Lakers last year claims he was robbed in a restroom at their home arena in a lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: What to expect when China's legislature meets this week
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China’s annual major political event gets underway this week, bringing together the nation’s top leaders and thousands of provincial leaders to endorse decisions already made by the all-powerful Chinese Communist Party.
item.dc:creator: EMILY WANG FUJIYAMA, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Trump hits 'pause' on US aid to Ukraine after Oval dustup, pressuring Zelenskyy on Russia talks
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Monday directed a “pause” to U.S. assistance to Ukraine after a disastrous Oval Office meeting as he seeks to pressure Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy to engage in peace talks with Russia.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Bill to ban trans athletes from girls' and women's sports teams fails to advance in Senate
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Legislation that aimed to bar transgender women and girls nationwide from participating in school athletic competitions designated for female athletes failed to advance Monday night in a divided Senate as Democrats stood united against an issue that Republicans leveraged in last ye
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Storms, possible twisters to threaten the South just as New Orleans celebrates Mardi Gras
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Powerful storms with a threat of tornadoes are expected to punch through Louisiana and other parts of the South on Tuesday just as costumed revelers celebrate Mardi Gras with huge parades and partying in the streets of New Orleans and other cities in the region.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Head of FBI New York office says he's retired from the bureau after being ordered to do so
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The head of the FBI's New York field office who was reported to have resisted Justice Department efforts to scrutinize agents who participated in politically sensitive investigations told coworkers on Monday that he has retired from the bureau after being directed to do so.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Michigan mom seeks 'respect' for her 3 missing boys, asks a judge to declare them dead
item.description: ADRIAN, Mich. (AP) — The mother of three missing Michigan boys asked a judge Monday to declare them dead nearly 15 years later, saying they deserve the respect that they didn't get at the end of their young lives.
item.dc:creator: ED WHITE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Slain California fire captain's fugitive wife 'scared' ex mother-in-law
item.description: The former mother-in-law of a woman accused of murdering her fire captain wife in Southern California said that she was "very scary.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New York City reports measles cases, raising public health concern
item.description: Measles is a highly contagious virus that can infect up to 90% of unvaccinated people who come in close proximity to an infected person, according to the New York City Department of Health.
item.dc:creator: Ben Mitchell, NewsNation

item.title: Universal Studios Orlando teases new attractions across its theme parks
item.description: Universal Studios Orlando teased that new projects for all its theme parks are in the works. In a video promoting its latest theme park, Epic Universe, Karen Irwin, president and COO of Universal Studios Orlando, hinted that more projects are in the works.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Hegseth orders iconic Georgia base to revert to Fort Benning, but with a twist
item.description: Fort Moore, a Georgia U.S. Army installation, will revert back to its old name, Fort Benning, on the orders of Secretary of Defense Pete Hegseth.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Popular clothing store chain slashing hundreds of workers, closing California HQ
item.description: One of the most well-known clothing brands in the United States, Forever 21, is laying off nearly 700 workers in California and Pennsylvania and closing its headquarters in the Golden State.
item.dc:creator: Marc Sternfield, KTLA 5

item.title: How did Americans view the Trump-Zelenskyy meeting? Poll shows stark divide
item.description: About half of those polled said neither Ukraine nor the U.S. walked away as a winner from last week's meeting.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Prices rose along border ahead of Trump's tariffs
item.description: As 25% tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada are set to take effect on Tuesday, Hispanic-owned businesses and companies that depend on cross-border trade are already passing higher prices onto consumers and preparing to sharply reduce imports.
item.dc:creator: FERNANDA FIGUEROA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Youngkin grants clemency to a fired Virginia police sergeant who shot and killed an unarmed man
item.description: RICHMOND, Va. (AP) — Days after a judge sentenced a Virginia police officer to prison in the fatal shooting of an unarmed man accused of stealing sunglasses, the state's Republican governor granted the fired officer clemency, meaning he won't have to serve further time behind bars.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Black graves are being moved to make way for an industrial park, drawing a mix of emotions
item.description: DANVILLE, Va. (AP) — A decision to move the remains of hundreds African American tenant farmers from a former Virginia tobacco plantation to a dedicated burial ground has elicited a range of emotions among the sharecroppers’ descendants.
item.dc:creator: BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Illinois couple's home explodes on wedding day; authorities say suspect was a guest's boyfriend
item.description: Authorities have identified the individual responsible for an explosion in Cicero that happened on Feb. 15 and left one person dead and 11 families displaced, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Ethan Illers, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Celtics honor Somerset teen who saved choking girl
item.description: Ben Courville, a junior firefighter with the Dighton Fire Department, saved a girl last summer from choking at a Fall River restaurant.
item.dc:creator: Melanie DaSilva, NewsNation

item.title: 2 men charged, nitrous oxide seized after investigation in Nashville
item.description: Officers reportedly seized more than 51,000 grams of nitrous oxide in cans, drug paraphernalia, marijuana and several hundred dollars in cash.
item.dc:creator: Aubriella Jackson, NewsNation

item.title: New charge filed against son of Buc-ee's co-founder
item.description: Mitchell Wasek, 29, is being charged with a third-degree felony offense of possession or promotion of child pornography, with a $50,000 bond.
item.dc:creator: Dalton Huey, NewsNation

item.title: JD Vance's role in Zelenskyy clash puts spotlight on VP
item.description: Vice President JD Vance's vocal involvement was among several noteworthy moments and led to mixed reactions.
item.dc:creator: Joe Khalil, NewsNation

item.title: California Highway Patrol blames drug use, speeding for fiery Cybertruck crash that killed 3
item.description: PIEDMONT, Calif. (AP) — Drug use and excessive speed were to blame for last year's fiery crash of a Tesla Cybertruck that killed three college students near San Francisco, the California Highway Patrol said in a preliminary report.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Chipotle will absorb tariff-related cost increases, CEO says
item.description: With the Trump administration's 25% tariff on all Mexican imports set to take effect Tuesday, Chipotle has pledged to absorb potential cost increases.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Delandro, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Education Department offers $25K for staffers to quit
item.description: “This is a one time offer in advance of a very significant Reduction in Force for the US Department of Education,” Jacqueline Clay said.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Kroger CEO resigns amid personal conduct investigation
item.description: McMullen's departure comes months after Kroger's failed two-year effort to merge with another supermarket giant, Albertsons.
item.dc:creator: Adam Conn, Fox 5

item.title: Oscars 2025: Michelle Trachtenberg, Shannen Doherty, more left out of In Memoriam segment
item.description: As is nearly always the case, a few famous faces were missing from the In Memoriam segment — and their fans were quick to notice.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: In Memoriam snubs: Late actors who weren't honored at the Oscars
item.description: Michelle Trachtenberg, Shannen Doherty, Tony Todd, and Quincy Jones were some of the notable figures not included in the In Memoriam segment at the 2024 Academy Awards, sparking outrage among their fans.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KTLA 5

item.title: Beloved LA staple Original Pantry Cafe closes after 100 years
item.description: The restaurant was owned by former L.A. Mayor Richard Riordan, and it was placed in a trust after his death in 2023.
item.dc:creator: Lily Dallow, NewsNation

item.title: Casey Anthony launches TikTok, Substack to ‘advocate’ for daughter she was accused of killing
item.description: Casey Anthony posted a TikTok video as part of a larger series in which she intends to speak about legal advocacy issues "as a proponent for the LGBTQ community" and "women's rights.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: This rare bean may be your future coffee as warming climate hurts crops
item.description: As leading coffee-producing countries struggle to grow crops in drier, less reliable weather, prices have soared to the highest in decades and the industry is scrambling for solutions.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Crews from across South Carolina help battle Carolina Forest-area wildfires
item.description: Regional and state fire rescue crews on Monday continued to battle the Carolina Forest-area wildfires, according to Horry County Fire Rescue.
item.dc:creator: Jordan White, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Feds bust massive alleged Guatemalan human smuggling ring operating out of California
item.description: Federal agents announced four Guatemalan men have been charged in connection with the bust of an alleged human smuggling operation in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump announces US crypto reserve
item.description: His announcement comes just after the White House announced it would be welcoming cryptocurrency industry professionals on March 7 in a first-of-its-kind summit.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 175 wildfires in North and South Carolina force some evacuations
item.description: Wildfires in North and South Carolina have scorched thousands of acres as crews battling more than 175 blazes face dry and windy conditions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man pleads guilty to murder and attempted murder in shooting at suburban Chicago July 4 parade
item.description: An Illinois man pleaded guilty Monday to killing seven people and injuring dozens more when he opened fire on a 2022 Independence Day parade in a Chicago suburb, a stunning development moments before opening statements in his trial on murder and attempted murder charges.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Tareen, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FedEx plane makes emergency landing after bird strike
item.description: “FedEx Flight 3609 from Newark to Indianapolis experienced a bird strike during takeoff," a FedEx spokesperson said.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, Fox 5
